title: magonia cumingii
intro: Magonia cumingii, also known as the Philippine madrono or Philippine mago, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is a tree with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to prepare herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and boosted immune system. In traditional medicine, Magonia cumingii is also used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, highlighting its versatility as a medicinal plant.
